engineIntelligence.h2: Porsche 944 Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Porsche 944 in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Porsche 944 M44/41 engine - petrol 3.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine suffers catastrophic valve/piston damage if belt snaps.
Balance shaft bearing wear - rubber bearings deteriorate causing vibration and oil starvation risk.
Reference sensor failure - flywheel grub screw/shear pin failure causes no-start condition.

cards.altText: Porsche 944 M44/51 engine - petrol 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger K26 failure - boost leaks and compressor wear common on high-mileage units.
Fuel pressure regulator damper failure - causes overfuelling, black smoke and poor running.
Head gasket failure - overheating from cooling system issues leads to costly repairs.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £6,500-£12,000 (supply only) Estimated from comparable M44/51 units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Porsche 944 M44/40 engine - petrol 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - interference design means belt snap destroys valves and pistons.
DME relay failure - classic no-start fault; relay cracks cause intermittent power loss.
AFM flap wear - air flow meter issues lead to hesitation, stalling and rich running.

engineGuide.h2: Porsche 944 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Porsche 944 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: Base 8v engine for early 944s; durable but prone to timing belt failure if neglected.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine suffers valve/piston damage if belt snaps (60k-80k miles)
DME relay failure - cracked solder joints cause intermittent no-start condition
Oil pressure sensor wear - false warnings or missed low-pressure alerts on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M44/09 Porsche 944 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Petrol
entries.history: Transitional 2.7L 8v unit for late 1988 models; improved torque over 2.5L base engine.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure - same interference risk as 2.5L; critical preventative maintenance item
Balance shaft bearing wear - rubber bearings degrade causing vibration and oil flow issues
Cooling system leaks - water pump and thermostat housing failures lead to overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for M44/10 Porsche 944 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol (Early Production)
entries.history: Earliest 944 engine code; 150hp output with simpler electronics pre-1985 facelift.
commonFailures: Reference sensor failure - flywheel grub screw/shear pin causes no-spark no-start
AFM flap wear - air flow meter issues lead to poor running and hesitation
Oil pump wear - lower pressure on high-mileage examples requires rebuild
entries.cta: Get quotes for M44/06 Porsche 944 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: High-revving DOHC 16v engine for the 944 S; prized for responsiveness but timing-critical.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure - interference design; belt snap destroys valves and pistons (50k-70k miles)
DME relay failure - classic no-start fault; relay cracks cause intermittent power loss
Valve stem seal wear - oil consumption increases after 70k miles on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M44/40 Porsche 944 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: The definitive 944 S2 engine; 3.0L 16v unit delivering smooth, torquey 211hp performance.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine; catastrophic damage if belt snaps (60k-80k miles)
Balance shaft bearing wear - rubber bearings deteriorate causing vibration and oil starvation
Reference sensor failure - flywheel grub screw/shear pin failure causes no-start condition
entries.cta: Get quotes for M44/41 Porsche 944 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol (968-Compatible)
entries.history: Later 3.0L 16v code shared with 968; technically compatible with 944 S2 with ECU adaptation.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure - same interference risk; preventative replacement essential
Variocam system wear - on later units, cam adjustment issues cause rough running
Cooling system leaks - water pump and thermostat housing failures on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for M44/42 Porsche 944 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: Iconic KKK K26 turbocharged unit; 220hp performance but complex maintenance requirements.
commonFailures: Turbocharger K26 failure - boost leaks and compressor wear common on high-mileage units
Fuel pressure regulator damper failure - causes overfuelling, black smoke and poor running
Head gasket failure - overheating from cooling system issues leads to costly repairs
entries.cta: Get quotes for M44/51 Porsche 944 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol Turbo S/SE
entries.history: Rare 250hp high-output Turbo S/SE engine; most desirable 944 powerplant for collectors.
commonFailures: Turbocharger K26 failure - high boost pressures accelerate wear on compressor/turbine wheels
Fuel system issues - high-pressure pump and damper failures cause overfuelling and black smoke
Bottom-end stress - high output increases rod bearing and crankshaft wear on track-used examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for M44/52 Porsche 944 engine replacement

Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Porsche 944 engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Porsche 944 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Porsche 944's M44 engine family is robust when maintained, but timing belt failure on these interference engines causes catastrophic damage. Other prominent issues include DME relay faults, balance shaft bearing wear, and turbocharger failures on 951 models. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Porsche 944 Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belt degrades with age and heat; if it snaps, pistons strike open valves on this interference design.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace belt, tensioners, water pump, seals before failure
Remove head, replace bent valves, guides, seals; machine head if warped
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block undamaged; risk of hidden cracks remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for catastrophic failure; restores reliability with warranty protection

problems.recommendation: On a Porsche 944 worth £8,000-£25,000, preventative timing belt replacement at 50k miles is far cheaper than post-failure repairs. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine from a specialist offers better long-term value than a head-only repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M44 engine prices for your Porsche 944

problems.h4: DME Relay Failure - Why Porsche 944 Won't Start
problems.affectedModels: Porsche 944 all variants (1982-1991, all M44 codes)
problems.rootCause: Solder joints crack in the Digital Motor Electronics relay due to thermal cycling, interrupting power to fuel pump and ignition.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace relay unit with new or refurbished part; diagnostic check
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix if relay is the sole fault; cheap and quick

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Check relay socket, fuse box, ground points for corrosion or damage
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses secondary electrical issues that may mimic relay failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Only if relay fault was misdiagnosed and engine damaged by other cause
repairOptions.longevity: Not applicable for relay fault alone; included for completeness

problems.recommendation: The DME relay is a £30-£80 part. Always test or replace this first on a no-start 944 before considering engine work. If the engine cranks but won't fire, this is the most likely culprit.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Porsche 944 electrical diagnostics and engine replacement

problems.h4: Balance Shaft Bearing Failure - Porsche 944 Vibration & Oil Pressure Loss
problems.rootCause: Rubber-mounted balance shaft bearings degrade, allowing shaft to contact housing; metal debris contaminates oil system.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drop sump, replace bearings, clean oil galleries; requires engine removal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before debris damages bearings or pump

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip engine, replace bearings, pistons, rings; machine crank if scored
repairOptions.longevity: Best if oil contamination has caused bearing wear but block is salvageable

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded balance shaft components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for high-mileage engines or severe contamination

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rhythmic knocking from the sump area on a high-mileage 944, stop driving immediately. A rebuilt engine with upgraded balance shaft components offers peace of mind versus a partial repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M44/41 engine prices for your Porsche 944 S2

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Porsche 944 Turbo Boost Loss & Black Smoke
problems.affectedModels: Porsche 944 Turbo, Turbo S (1986-1988, M44/51, M44/52)
problems.rootCause: KKK K26 turbo bearings wear from heat and oil coking; compressor wheel damage causes boost loss and oil burning.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace bearings/seals, balance rotating assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if housing and wheels are undamaged; requires specialist turbo shop

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit refurbished K26 exchange turbo with warranty; includes gaskets and lines
repairOptions.longevity: Best balance of cost and reliability for most owners

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with new/rebuilt turbo, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage Turbo engines or if bottom-end is also worn

problems.recommendation: On a 944 Turbo, always inspect the turbo and fuel system together. A rebuilt engine with a new turbo offers the most reliable long-term solution for high-mileage examples.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M44/51 Turbo engine prices for your Porsche 944

problems.h4: Reference Sensor Failure - Porsche 944 No-Spark No-Start
problems.affectedModels: Porsche 944 all variants (1982-1991, all M44 codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Age-related; common on cars stored or with high vibration
problems.rootCause: Flywheel reference sensor grub screw/shear pin fails, preventing crank position signal to DME; engine cranks but won't fire.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove starter, inspect flywheel, replace shear pin/grub screw and sensor
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix if flywheel undamaged; quick and cost-effective

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace flywheel if pin hole is elongated or damaged; includes labour
repairOptions.longevity: Required if reference pin damage has compromised flywheel integrity

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Only if sensor fault was misdiagnosed and engine damaged by other cause
repairOptions.longevity: Not applicable for sensor fault alone; included for completeness

problems.recommendation: A no-start 944 that cranks normally is far more likely to have a reference sensor or DME relay fault than internal engine damage. Diagnose these cheap fixes first.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Porsche 944 no-start diagnostics and engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Porsche 944
finalCta.paragraph: The Porsche 944 is a rewarding classic, but its interference engines demand proactive maintenance. When failure strikes, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Porsche 944 specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Porsche 944 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Porsche 944 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Porsche 944 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Porsche 944 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5-litre M44 engine powered the base 944 from 1982-1987 and the Turbo models through 1988, offering durable performance but requiring strict timing belt maintenance on this interference design.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure on interference engines; DME relay

items.title: Porsche 944 2.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The transitional 2.7-litre 8-valve engine appeared in late 1988 models, offering improved torque over the 2.5L base unit while retaining the same maintenance-critical timing belt design.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure; balance shaft bearing wear on higher-mileage examples

items.title: Porsche 944 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The definitive 3.0-litre 16-valve M44/41 engine powered the 944 S2 from 1989-1991, delivering smooth 211hp performance but sharing the interference design and balance shaft bearing vulnerability of earlier M44 units.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure; balance shaft bearing wear; reference sensor shear pin failure

engineYears.h2: Porsche 944 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Porsche 944's production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original 944 launched with the 150hp M44/09 2.5L 8-valve engine, establishing the model's durable but maintenance-critical character.
keyChanges: Launch of Porsche 944 with 2.5L M44/09 engine (150hp)
Introduction of transaxle layout for balanced weight distribution
Early models used simpler electronics pre-1985.5 facelift
knownFor: Durable cast-iron block but interference engine design
DME relay and reference sensor faults common on aged examples
Timing belt critical - failure causes catastrophic valve damage
Simple electronics make DIY diagnosis possible for electrical faults
years.cta: Get quotes for 1982-1985 Porsche 944 replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche 944 2.5 engine replacement • M44/09 engine for sale • 944 timing belt failure cost

years.description: The 1985.5 facelift brought increased power (163hp) and updated electronics, with the M44/09 engine remaining the core powerplant.
keyChanges: Power increase to 163hp via revised intake and ECU mapping
Updated dashboard and electronics for 1985.5+ models
Introduction of catalytic converter variants for emissions compliance
knownFor: Improved reliability over earliest models but same interference risk
Catalytic converter models require careful fuel mixture management
AFM flap wear begins to affect running on higher-mileage examples
Still prone to DME relay failure as units age
years.cta: Get quotes for 1985.5-1987 Porsche 944 replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche 944 1985 engine • M44/09 replacement cost • 944 no-start DME relay fix

years.description: The iconic 944 Turbo (951) arrived with the KKK K26 turbocharged M44/51 engine, delivering 220hp and defining the model's performance legacy.
keyChanges: Introduction of 944 Turbo with M44/51 2.5L turbo engine (220hp)
KKK K26 turbocharger and upgraded cooling system
1988 saw limited-run Turbo S/SE with M44/52 250hp engine
knownFor: Turbo models demand meticulous maintenance of boost system and cooling
Fuel pressure regulator damper failure causes black smoke and poor running
K26 turbo wear manifests as boost loss, oil consumption or compressor noise
High-performance use accelerates bottom-end wear on track examples

years.description: The 944 S introduced the high-revving M44/40 2.5L 16-valve engine, delivering 189-190hp and enthusiast appeal but with critical timing belt requirements.
keyChanges: Launch of 944 S with DOHC M44/40 16-valve engine (189-190hp)
Dual overhead cams and revised cylinder head for higher output
Shared interference design with base 8v but higher performance envelope
knownFor: High-revving character but same interference timing belt risk
Valve stem seal wear increases oil consumption on high-mileage units
DME relay and reference sensor faults remain common electrical issues
Enthusiast favourite but requires strict maintenance discipline

Revised block, crank and heads for increased displacement
Final production year 1991 marked end of 944 line before 968 succession
knownFor: Smooth, torquey delivery but same interference timing belt risk
Balance shaft bearing wear a known high-mileage failure point
Reference sensor shear pin failure causes no-start on aged examples
Most desirable naturally aspirated 944 for collectors and drivers

faq.h2: Porsche 944 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Porsche 944 engine in the UK?
items.answer: Replacing a Porsche 944 engine typically costs £1,500-£4,500 for a used unit or £4,200-£12,000 for a professionally rebuilt engine, depending on variant and engine code. Supply and fit labour adds £800-£2,200.
keyPoints: Used engines: £1,500-£4,500 supply only
Rebuilt engines: £4,200-£12,000 supply only
Turbo and S2 engines command premium pricing
Labour for supply and fit: £800-£2,200 additional
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Porsche 944 engine prices for your variant

items.question: What causes a Porsche 944 engine to fail catastrophically?
items.answer: Timing belt failure is the most catastrophic Porsche 944 engine failure, as all M44 engines are interference design; snapped belts destroy valves and pistons. DME relay and reference sensor faults cause no-start but not internal damage.
keyPoints: Timing belt failure destroys valves/pistons on interference engines
Replace timing belt every 50k miles or 5 years preventatively
DME relay and reference sensor cause no-start, not mechanical damage
Balance shaft bearing wear can contaminate oil system if ignored
items.cta: Get quotes for Porsche 944 timing belt replacement and engine rebuild

items.question: Which Porsche 944 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The 3.0L M44/41 engine in the 944 S2 is widely regarded as the most reliable naturally aspirated 944 engine, offering smooth power and robust construction when maintained. Early 2.5L 8v units are also durable if timing belts are replaced on schedule.
keyPoints: M44/41 3.0L S2 engine offers best balance of power and reliability
All M44 engines require strict timing belt maintenance
Turbo models (M44/51/52) need careful boost system care
Regular oil changes with correct viscosity critical for longevity
items.cta: Compare Porsche 944 S2 engine prices for reliable replacement

items.question: Can I fit a 968 engine in my Porsche 944 S2?
items.answer: Yes, the 968's M44/42 3.0L engine is largely compatible with the 944 S2's M44/41, sharing block architecture and mounting points, but requires ECU and wiring adaptation for full functionality.
keyPoints: M44/42 (968) and M44/41 (944 S2) share core architecture
ECU, wiring harness and sensor adaptation required
Physical fitment is straightforward for experienced specialists
Performance gains possible with 968 management system swap
items.cta: Enquire about Porsche 944 S2 3.0L engine compatibility and pricing

items.question: Why won't my Porsche 944 start after cutting out while driving?
items.answer: A Porsche 944 that cuts out and won't restart is most often caused by DME relay failure or reference sensor shear pin failure, not internal engine damage. Both are relatively inexpensive electrical fixes.
keyPoints: DME relay failure interrupts power to fuel pump and ignition
Reference sensor shear pin failure prevents crank position signal
Both faults cause crank-but-no-start, not mechanical seizure
Test/replace relay and inspect reference sensor before engine work
items.cta: Get quotes for Porsche 944 no-start diagnostics and engine replacement

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding a Porsche 944 engine or buying used?
items.answer: Rebuilding a Porsche 944 engine is worth it for long-term ownership or high-value variants like Turbo S/S2; used engines suit budget projects or short-term ownership but carry unknown history risks.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines offer warranty and upgraded failure-point components
Used engines cheaper but require compression/oil pressure verification
Turbo and S2 variants justify rebuild investment due to collector value
Factor in labour costs and downtime when comparing options
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Porsche 944 engine prices and warranties

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Porsche 944 engine?
items.answer: Replacement Porsche 944 engines sourced through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt and reconditioned units; used engines typically carry shorter 3-6 month coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/reconditioned: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Used engines: 3-6 month warranty typical, varies by supplier
Warranty covers parts and workmanship, not misuse or installation error
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Find Porsche 944 engine suppliers with 12-month warranty coverage

items.question: How long does a Porsche 944 engine replacement take?
items.answer: A Porsche 944 engine replacement typically takes 2-4 weeks from order to completion, including sourcing the engine, shipping, and specialist fitting; rebuilt engines may take longer due to machining time.
keyPoints: Sourcing and shipping: 3-10 days depending on availability
Specialist fitting: 2-4 days labour for experienced Porsche technicians
Rebuilt engines may require 1-2 weeks machining time before dispatch
Allow buffer for ECU adaptation and post-installation testing
items.cta: Get quotes for Porsche 944 engine supply and fit with timeline estimate

items.question: What oil should I use in a Porsche 944 engine?
items.answer: Porsche 944 engines require high-quality mineral or semi-synthetic 10W-40 or 20W-50 oil meeting Porsche A40 specification; avoid thin modern synthetics that may not protect older bearing clearances.
keyPoints: Use 10W-40 or 20W-50 mineral/semi-synthetic meeting Porsche A40
Avoid thin 0W-20/5W-30 modern synthetics on high-mileage engines
Change oil every 5,000-7,500 miles or annually, whichever comes first
Correct oil critical for balance shaft bearing and turbo longevity
items.cta: Compare Porsche 944 engine replacement options with maintenance guidance

items.question: Do you supply and fit Porsche 944 engines nationwide?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket connects you with UK-wide Porsche 944 specialists offering complete supply and fit engine replacement services, including collection, installation, and post-fit testing with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Network of vetted Porsche specialist garages across the UK
Supply and fit includes engine, labour, fluids and post-installation testing
Nationwide collection and delivery options available
Single-point warranty coverage for parts and labour through EngineMarket
items.cta: Find Porsche 944 engine supply and fit specialists near you
